dailyJudgementSnippet: OpenAI won the day because HN treated the end of Microsoft's exclusive tie as a release valve: commenters said the old deal had been kneecapping OpenAI and liked that it could now choose infrastructure more freely [E1].
winnerExplanation: OpenAI took the top spot on the strength of the day's biggest story and a clearly favorable read in the comments. The discussion centered on OpenAI escaping an arrangement people thought had limited its cloud options and bargaining power, giving it the strongest direct positive signal of the day [E1].

openai.judgementSnippet: Ending the exclusive Microsoft tie was read as removing a constraint that had been holding OpenAI back, especially around cloud choice and leverage [E1].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Anthropic showed up mostly as a comparison point: one top comment framed it as serious enough competition to pressure the Microsoft/OpenAI relationship, while another used Sonnet as a near-peer in a harness-vs-model discussion [E5][E6].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Gemini got credit for powering a strong TerminalBench result, but commenters repeatedly said the harness explained much of the jump; Gemini Nano in Chrome also drew interest for free local inference, alongside complaints about download size and weaker quality than newer small Google models [E2][E3].

microsoft_copilot.judgementSnippet: Usage-based billing was attacked as an effective price hike with poor monthly-value tradeoffs and extreme model multipliers, and several commenters said they would switch to PAYG providers or alternatives [E4].

evidence.summary: In the top-ranked Microsoft/OpenAI breakup story, comments framed the old Azure exclusivity and revenue share as having 'kneecapped' OpenAI and welcomed its new freedom to choose other clouds and hardware.

The Show HN centered on an agent posting its best TerminalBench result on Gemini-3-flash-preview; commenters acknowledged the strong number but argued harness design and possible overfitting explained much of it.
Chrome's Prompt API was discussed as using Gemini Nano; comments liked free, privacy-preserving local inference but noted the large model download and argued newer small Google models seemed better.
GitHub Copilot's move to usage-based billing was widely criticized as a price increase with no monthly-value upside, harsh model multipliers, and clear switching intent from commenters.
A top comment on the Microsoft/OpenAI story treated Anthropic as serious competition strong enough to threaten Microsoft's OpenAI investment and help explain why the agreement was loosened.

In the Gemini TerminalBench thread, a commenter said swapping Gemini for Sonnet changed results less than swapping the harness, using Anthropic mainly as a parity comparison rather than a separate win or loss.
